title: The Customs Receipt
description: Inside the East India Company crate on the Customs Dock, beneath a layer of straw packing, you find a folded receipt on official customs paper. It records a payment of thirty pounds sterling to a 'confidential informant' and is countersigned by Agent Silas Dawes. The informant is not named — but a second document beneath it is a brief acknowledgment note, signed with the initials T.G. in the same precise handwriting as the coded letter you may have already found.
